title: Make a Basic First Aid Kit
summary: Assemble a small first aid kit with essential supplies for minor injuries.
learnerPromise: You will be able to gather and organize the basic supplies needed to treat small cuts, scrapes, and minor burns at home or on the go.
whyItMatters: Having a simple first aid kit ready means you can respond quickly to small injuries instead of searching for supplies when you need them.
realLifeUses: Treating a scraped knee after a fall at home
Cleaning and covering a small cut while on a day trip
Managing a minor burn from a hot pan or curling tool
Helping a friend who gets a small injury while you are together
materialsNeeded: Small box, pouch, or zip-top bag
Adhesive bandages in a few sizes
Sterile gauze pads or clean cloth squares
Optional: instant cold pack, scissors, mild soap
result: A portable first aid kit organized and ready to use for minor injuries.
youWillLearn: Choose supplies that match the most common minor injuries you might face
Organize items so they are easy to grab quickly
Store the kit in a clean, dry, easy-to-reach place
Check expiration dates and replace used items
miniChallenge: Gather the basic supplies listed above and pack them into a container. Label it and decide where to store it.
steps: Wash your hands before handling any supplies.
Choose a clean, dry container that closes securely.
Place bandages, gauze, tape, wipes, tweezers, and gloves inside.
Write a small list of what is inside and tape it to the lid.
Label the kit and store it somewhere everyone in your space knows about.
completionCriteria: Your kit contains at least bandages, gauze, tape, antiseptic wipes, tweezers, and gloves
The kit is labeled and stored in an agreed-upon location
You can name at least two situations where you would use the kit
commonProblems: Supplies expire or dry out if not checked regularly
Items get used and not replaced, leaving the kit empty over time
The kit gets stored in a hard-to-reach spot during an urgent moment
tips: Check your kit every three to six months and replace anything expired or missing
Keep a smaller version with just bandages and wipes in your bag for outings
Include a basic instruction card inside if you are still learning how to use each item
helpBoundary: Ask a medical professional or trained first aid instructor if you are unsure what supplies you need, how to use an item, or whether a wound needs more than basic care.
